# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;

class WebsitePreviewController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Fetch and display a website preview.
     *
     * @param  \Illuminate\Http\Request  $request
     * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response
     */
    public function preview(Request $request)
    {
        $request->validate([
            'url' => 'required|url',
        ]);

        $url = $request->input('url');
        $content = '';
        $statusCode = 0;
        $errorMessage = '';

        $ch = curl_init();

        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, $url);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, 1);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS, 5); // Limit redirects
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_TIMEOUT, 10); // 10 second timeout
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, false); // For development, consider true in production with proper CA certs
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST, false); // For development, consider 2 in production
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_USERAGENT, 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'); // Mimic a browser

        $content = curl_exec($ch);
        $statusCode = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);

        if (curl_errno($ch)) {
            $errorMessage = 'cURL Error: ' . curl_error($ch);
            Log::error("Website preview cURL error for URL: {$url} - {$errorMessage}");
        }

        curl_close($ch);

        if ($statusCode >= 400 || $content === false) {
            $errorMessage = $errorMessage ?: "Failed to fetch content. HTTP Status Code: {$statusCode}";
            Log::warning("Failed to fetch content for URL: {$url}. Status: {$statusCode}. Error: {$errorMessage}");
            return response()->json([
                'error' => true,
                'message' => $errorMessage,
                'status_code' => $statusCode,
                'url' => $url,
            ], $statusCode >= 400 ? $statusCode : 500);
        }

        // Basic parsing for common meta tags (title, description, image)
        // This is a simplified approach. For robust parsing, consider using a dedicated library like Goutte (Symfony DomCrawler).
        $title = '';
        $description = '';
        $image = '';
        $domain = parse_url($url, PHP_URL_HOST);

        if (!empty($content)) {
            $dom = new \DOMDocument();
            @$dom->loadHTML($content); // Use @ to suppress HTML parsing warnings

            $xpath = new \DOMXPath($dom);

            // Get title
            $titleNode = $xpath->query('//meta[@property="og:title"]/@content')->item(0)
                        ?: $xpath->query('//title')->item(0);
            $title = $titleNode ? ($titleNode instanceof \DOMAttr ? $titleNode->value : $titleNode->textContent) : '';

            // Get description
            $descriptionNode = $xpath->query('//meta[@property="og:description"]/@content')->item(0)
                                ?: $xpath->query('//meta[@name="description"]/@content')->item(0);
            $description = $descriptionNode ? $descriptionNode->value : '';

            // Get image
            $imageNode = $xpath->query('//meta[@property="og:image"]/@content')->item(0);
            $image = $imageNode ? $imageNode->value : '';

            // Fallback for image: first img tag
            if (empty($image)) {
                $imgNode = $xpath->query('//img')->item(0);
                if ($imgNode && $imgNode->hasAttribute('src')) {
                    $src = $imgNode->getAttribute('src');
                    // Make sure the image URL is absolute
                    if (!preg_match('/^https?:\/\//i', $src)) {
                        $base = parse_url($url);
                        $scheme = isset($base['scheme']) ? $base['scheme'] : 'http';
                        $host = isset($base['host']) ? $base['host'] : '';
                        $path = isset($base['path']) ? dirname($base['path']) : '';

                        if (substr($src, 0, 2) === '//') { // Protocol-relative
                            $image = $scheme . ':' . $src;
                        } elseif (substr($src, 0, 1) === '/') { // Absolute path
                            $image = $scheme . '://' . $host . $src;
                        } else { // Relative path
                            $image = $scheme . '://' . $host . ($path == '/' ? '' : $path) . '/' . $src;
                        }
                    } else {
                        $image = $src;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        return response()->json([
            'success' => true,
            'url' => $url,
            'title' => $title,
            'description' => $description,
            'image' => $image,
            'domain' => $domain,
            'status_code' => $statusCode,
        ]);
    }
}
